project.name: The Antiqued Map
project.description: A short scene where a cataloger finds a hidden 1742 map under a writing desk. The clues argue against forgery and point toward recent concealment — by someone who knew the desk well.

layers.label: Characters
layers.description: People present, named, or referred to.

layers.label: Objects
layers.description: Physical artifacts that bear weight in the scene.

layers.label: Events
layers.description: Things that happen, anchored in time.

layers.label: Inferences
layers.description: Conclusions and hypotheses, with their supports/contradictions.

nodes.name: Eliot
nodes.summary: Estate cataloger; the protagonist. Tired, methodical, alone past midnight.

nodes.name: Mrs. Halloran
nodes.summary: Recently deceased; owner of the estate being cataloged; collector of oddities.

nodes.name: Tobias Halloran
nodes.summary: Mrs. Halloran's late husband; known prankster among maritime societies — but never about his own desk.

nodes.name: Reuben Halloran
nodes.summary: Tobias's uncle; captain of the Halcyon; vanished off Marblehead in 1849.

nodes.name: Joseph Blanchard
nodes.summary: 1742 cartographer; signed the front of the map.

nodes.name: The 1742 chart
nodes.summary: Hand-tinted Blanchard chart of the New England coast; verso has an X and an annotation in a different ink.

nodes.name: Mrs. Halloran's writing desk
nodes.summary: Has a concealed compartment under the bottom drawer; the desk was originally Tobias's.

nodes.name: The Halcyon
nodes.summary: Brigantine captained by Reuben; vanished off Marblehead in 1849.

nodes.name: The shop
nodes.summary: Closed at eight; where Eliot has been cataloging past midnight.

nodes.name: Marblehead
nodes.summary: Coast where the X is marked and where the Halcyon vanished.

nodes.name: Port of Salem
nodes.summary: Where the map's paper imports came through in 1740-1745, per the watermark.

nodes.name: Map signed (1742)
nodes.summary: Blanchard signs the front of the chart.

nodes.name: Halcyon vanishes (1849)
nodes.summary: Reuben's brigantine disappears off Marblehead — context for the verso annotation.

nodes.name: Midnight discovery
nodes.summary: After three days of cataloging, Eliot finds the map in the concealed compartment.

nodes.name: The X on the verso
nodes.summary: Small, deliberate X marking a location off Marblehead.

nodes.name: The Reuben annotation
nodes.summary: "Three fathoms beneath, ask Reuben." — written in a different ink than the map proper.

nodes.name: Iron gall ink (front)
nodes.summary: Authenticates Blanchard's signature for the 1742 era.

nodes.name: Walnut-and-soot ink (verso)
nodes.summary: Period-correct ink mix that a Tobias-era forger wouldn't have known to use.

nodes.name: 1740-45 Salem watermark
nodes.summary: Eliot matched it against three known Blanchard charts that afternoon — paper is authentic.

nodes.name: Concealed compartment
nodes.summary: Under the bottom drawer of the desk; the desk was Tobias's; Tobias was never a prankster about his own desk.

nodes.name: Forgery — rejected
nodes.summary: Three flaws: the verso ink, the watermark, and the desk's provenance all argue against forgery.

nodes.name: Recent hider
nodes.summary: Someone — not Tobias — placed the map in the compartment recently. Who, and why ask Reuben?

edges.description: Original owner; the concealed compartment was his.
Matched against three known Blanchard charts.
A note addressed to someone long-dead reads as a deliberate breadcrumb.

tour.narrative: Start with the moment — past midnight, after three days of cataloging, Eliot finds the map.
Examine the map itself: a 1742 Blanchard chart, hand-tinted, signed; verso carries an X off Marblehead.
The strange annotation: "Three fathoms beneath, ask Reuben." Different ink than the chart.
Who is Reuben? Tobias's uncle; captain of the Halcyon; vanished off Marblehead in 1849.
Why isn't this a forgery? Three flaws: ink mix, watermark, and desk provenance.
The inference that lands: someone hid this, recently. The question moves from "is this real?" to "who put it here, and why now?"
